reviews.review_description: In February, the house where my grandmother and uncle once lived was demolished. It was a place filled with happy memories, where I spent many long vacations as a child. Just before it was torn down, I captured every corner of the house using Hyperscape Capture. Even now, through my Meta Quest, I can still visit the living room where I once sat together with them. I only hope these memories will continue to live on for many years to come.

This app is incredible, but it is all stored in the cloud. I need to be able to actually download these spaces that I have scanned. It is awesome to be able to freeze my house in time and go back and see what it used to look like. When I am much much older, these scans will mean everything to me

My only question is when will we be able to download and use our spaces for actual things? This whole scan the space and relive it is all fun ,and if you want to be a knob you can "invite" people. But those are all novelty things that do nothing. I either need to be able to download my things I scanned or a app that actually does this for me. Get actual use from this than just being another dumb vr novelty

I scanned my room in the house I already left, and now it won't open! It's in the list, but nothing happens when I try to open it! And the old worlds are still accessible. How can I use the app if I'm not sure if what I scanned will be available to me tomorrow?

I can scan and upload my environment, but when I try to open these environments, nothing happened. It stuck in that loading scene for 10-30sec and then it just quit. I can't even open those environments in preview.

I take back what I said about the lack of quality with the beta app and thank Meta for migrating my preview app scans with very minimal loss of detail. Now just need to be able to share worlds to others mobile devices
Since yesterday when my headset updated to the new navigator (possibly related?) the first two of my three scans will load half way and then the app will crash. Worked fine right before that.

Updated Review (originally written in September 2025)

I am updating my review from September 2025. Since then, some improvements have been made, such as the ability to share environments, which is a positive and much-needed addition.

However, my core criticism at the time was very clear and remains critical: if users are not allowed to create backups, the service itself must be reliable over a span of years. That long-term reliability is the only thing that can justify preventing users from owning their own data, especially for a product positioned around capturing and preserving memories.

Unfortunately, this concern has now been confirmed in the worst possible way. With the latest update, worlds were removed, and I lost captures of places that no longer exist. It did not take years for this risk to materialize. It took two months.

With no backup or export options, those memories are permanently gone. This directly proves the original point: without backups, long-term persistence is not a nice-to-have—it is mandatory.

Despite the impressive capture technology and recent feature improvements, this single issue completely undermines trust in the platform. Without true data ownership or strong guarantees of continuity over time, the app cannot be considered a reliable tool for preserving meaningful memories.

developer_response.body: Thank you Kite_Numezawa for sharing your feedback using Hyperscape to preserve memories! You can still view all your previous captured Hyperscape worlds in the Preview app. No captures have been removed. As part of bringing Hyperscape in Horizon and allowing people to share their worlds with others, we now display only shareable Hyperscape worlds in the Capture app.

reviews.review_description: ...as others mentioned: you can't download or reuse the models which makes the app pretty useless. You test it once but never reuse it.

I am unable to go to any worlds in the capture app. I can view the featured rooms in the preview app but not the same ones in the capture app. I received a message that my capture world was ready to visit, but I just get an error message “worlds keeps stopping”.

reviews.review_title: Not able to capture privates space
reviews.review_description: Not able to capture private space and have it locally on my device which is totally against privacy :(
Quest sales will skyrocket from being a useful capture device in productivity workflows. Meta only shouldn’t do this if they hate money (seems to be the case).

I tried making my own world for the first time in there because I thought it would be cool but after trying to create my first world the whole thing froze my headset and I had to reset it after that I continued to make only my room and it worked after that I decided I would try to launch it later when it was finished processing when I try launching it a continuously crashes and does anything but work at one point I think it literally broke my headset and I couldn't even bring my menu up and I had to find a workaround. With all of this in mind the only thing I can preview is the stuff in the preview app other than that I can't do anything else as a user I think this is foul and the app should work perfectly or at least semi work I mean I can't even get into my own world that I created that's ridiculous I hope meta can fix this as soon as possible I'm not the only one that's experienced this problem 😐

My space was not generated. At the end, said 'failed from our end' do it again. 
Seriously???

Used the new beta to scan a new environment. worked fine. new environment accessible in capter beta and all my previous envs accessible through old preview app. then scanned a second new environment en capture beta... after confirmation of successuful render, both, the new capture beta and the okd preview app point to my old 'preview' environments. the new worlds (demos and my oen first) scan are no more accessible since the capture app now also points to the preview environment...

The initial preview had impressive capturing of details, but with recent updates to allow captures to be brought into spaces, the visual fidelity is much much lower. And with this level low level of quality, it doesn’t justify spending time and effort capturing a scene meticulously anymore

reviews.review_title: Please do option Import and Export
reviews.review_description:
With the new update I also lost my old scans of room that no longer exist. 
It is critical important that this application have options for import and export scans. 
Also old version have much more good texture quality and new version it to much blur and all low resolution. We need options of how big HD texture we want. Please add this new options in future update.
My last scan doesn't load since the forced update of my quest...
Good app but one thing Audio 🔉 when people go in we can't hear one another always

reviews.review_title: Why can’t we save a copy on our device?
reviews.review_description: I have terrible internet and the rooms that I’ve scanned are unusable because of it. Why can’t we save a scan locally? Is there a way to back up scans? What’s point if you can’t save your work?

While it is true that others can now be invited into the same world, the visual fidelity is simply too low.
The experience feels closer to a flat-shaded, anime-like environment that only approximates reality, rather than reproducing it.
As a result, realism has been largely lost.
I hope the system will soon allow world sharing at the same quality level as the initial preview.

For me, this would be a five star app, if I could export the scan and save it locally, especially since I'm using it to scan things that will no longer be around in a short period of time. The interface is good, the scans come out very nice, considering the camera quality, but without a way to export the scans, it's just a fancy toy that can break, outside of your control, at any moment. 

Once I can export, I'll change the review to five stars.

reviews.review_title: Spawning under the floor of my room
reviews.review_description:
Spent 35 minutes scanning a room in full detail.  Waited hours for it to be "processed".  And now, when I go into the room, I spawn under the floor.  Every time.
    I've cleared my boundaries and spatial history... recreated a stationary boundary and a roomscale boundary... and still am spawning under the floor.
     I can "jump" and see the room clearly for a split-second, but there's no way to "climb" up and be in the room instead of under the floor.
      Yes, the "floor" is set correctly in my boundaries.  Yes, I've restarted the Quest 3 and restarted the Hyperscape app numerous times.
    Absolutely frustrating.

Yes it makes great scans...
BUT! You can do nothing with it!
I can see tons of oportuneties IF you would free the files.
Archeolegie, archetecture, selling cars houses, tourism,..
You can make it a useable 3d scanner and then you can print out your family or your car...
You could invite people in VR in your place snd implement games ...or just a god d... chair...
Why isn't all that happening.
That could be the breakthrough...
Not that ugly Metaverse!

reviews.review_title: The Best Spatial Reality App So Far.
reviews.review_description:
Ever since I first became aware of spatial reality capture I have been experimenting with all the available apps to create real world experiences to be best viewed in VR but can also be enjoyed in 2D. The META Team has definitely come up with a great way of shepherding people to create excellent spatial reality captures using a mesh that must be dispersed with to make sure there is enough coverage to create an seamless virtual environment. What a brilliant addition to reality scan creations to make this App almost idiot proof for the novice creator.

So far all my captures have been excellent and I can't wait for the META team to improve the app and to allow us to share our creations with other creators online. This is so superior to what we get in the usual boring Horizon Worlds app that I hope META will push this forward and add it the META World experience so that we can all enjoy more vibrant and individual virtual Reality based environments.
Meta: please let us transfer our scans from the preview app! I scanned a room in another city (continent) that it’s unlikely I’ll visit again anytime soon. It looks amazing in the preview app.

i did my first test run today, and mapped a kitchen.  I want to go further, i need saves and the ability to share this with other meta users.  Your spatial mapping far exceeded my user level and I need more saves and time to extensively map stuff.  Thankfully the quest 3 is a capable device and I pray Meta keeps developing this apps and others more that make this such a great tool to have, and share with others.
How to create offline copy?
It is recommended to launch the local offline operation function.
I can not see any share or invite icon anywhere. I newly scanned a place and still no share icon.

reviews.review_title: Good for making scans, bad for quality
reviews.review_description: I gotta say this app is really neat and fun to use, but half the time whenever I put like 8 minutes into scanning a room with detail it either comes out blurry or my ceiling/wall is messed up, this happens to me almost everytime I use the app, my scans always come out blurry and distorted, if this could be fixed it would mean a lot.

I did some scans of rooms in my mums house on the beta hyperscape but just wondering if you can download them because now the new version is out it's saying I have no worlds but on the beta I go on I have 6 or 7. Do I have to do them again?
ANY CHANCE WE CAN DOWNLOAD THE EXPERIENCES  ALREADY SCANNED TO THE NEW VERSION PLEASE? MY SCANS ARE REALLY GOOD AND DONT REALLY WANT TO TRY HAVE TO DO THEM AGAIN??

Ich war ein grosser Fan der ersten Version. Das Upgrade von November 2025 macht einiges falsch. Sharing ist eine logische Funktionalität die noch gefehlt hat, aber einige Aspekte haben sehr gelitten:

- Die visuelle Qualität der Gaussian Splats hat gefühlt doch abgenommen. Das ist ausgesprochen schade.
- Die Navigation in der App ist entweder kaputt, oder nicht intuitiv. 
- Alte Datensätze sind in dieser App nicht mehr verfügbar.

Natürlich hat Early Access seine Nachteile. Aber schlechtere Qualitaet der Daten, schlechtes UX und Datenverlust? Ich hoffe zukünftige Versionen machen das besser. Diese App hat nämlich sehr viel Potential.

My only complaint is that once created, a scan is only available for 18+ users. Our VR play group was excited to virtually visit each other's houses, but my son was left out due to the restriction.  Other than that it was great!

Hyperscape has so much potential. I really like being able to share the models. However, the last update changed the quality of the scans for the worse. 
Please go back to the old quality gaussian splatting, or make a high quality version of the app, that you can also share with friends. 
I can see that there might be an advantage of the low res gaussian splatting for mobile or some use cases, but the real potential lies in the high quality scans that looked almost like the real world.

I can’t wait for the future and updates from this app, it’s amazing! Great to show friends or family from far away your spaces and be the closest to reality to being in a same space at the same time! 

Not giving 5 stars bescause the scanning takes long and crashes sometimes making you have to rescan …a long task…hopefully this gets fixed, but sooo worth it.
It is amazing but Meta del tés your files without warning and there is no way to download them to your device.

reviews.review_title: Sun lit room or studio lighting needed
reviews.review_description: I did a test scan last night which looks pretty much how expected to look I have a lot of dark furniture that doesn't stand out as much. if you're scanning just by one room light you want to have either more lights on or a studio light and dark environments. It's better to do this during sunny days and let light into the windows.

Hyperscape is very realistic and does an impressive job capturing real-world spaces with high visual accuracy. The scans feel immersive, and the final result genuinely looks like a true-to-life digital replica of your environment — which is pretty amazing.

What we’d love to see in future updates is more interactivity and creative freedom within the captured space. For example:

Bringing objects into the world (browser screens, media panels, or apps)

Interactive elements like a chessboard or games (darts, cards, etc.) to play with others

A movable or free-roaming camera for in-space streaming or content creation , or a mirror like we could use in Horizon worlds

More tools for social interaction and shared experiences

The app itself works very well overall. Rendering can take a while depending on the size and complexity of the space, but that’s understandable given the level of detail being processed.

All in all — wow. It’s an amazing foundation with huge potential. With more interactive and social features, this could easily become something next-level.
Keep advancing please, we need to record outdoor spaces like castles and sounds. And also ways to share with people who arent just friends.

reviews.review_title: Wow. I've been waiting for this 🤩
reviews.review_description: Amazing. Anyone not scoring this 5/5 is mad, or doing it wrong. I've been experimenting with different capture methods, trying to preserve the memory of my recently departed parents' family home before it is sold so I can share the space with my siblings in years to come. Tried on my own place today, before hopefully capturing my parents next weekend...WOW!! Nothing compares to this, it is simply the best option out there bar none. If you spend the time capturing, YOU WILL BE AMAZED AT THE RESULTS. Thank you Meta, truly.

I was excited to try this. I was able to scan multiple rooms in my house successfully. Then when I tried to upload it was incredibly slow and eventually showed failed to upload. It does not allow me to reupload, despite saying ready to upload, no matter how many headset  restarts or clicking it. I then tried to do a smaller room, scanned it ok, but then once again. Failed to upload, ready to upload. No option to execute the command despite headset restarts, app restarts.  Hope they can fix these bugs soon..

Haven't been able to use the app because every single time I try to scan I ALWAYS get an error saying the scan was interupted and couldn't be completed. I could complete the scan if it would stop"interupting" the scan with an error and actually let me finish the scan!

Initially I was unable to install this, but as of yesterday it's working.  And I'm blown away, truly.  Just ... wow. I scanned one room of my house, and I'm thrilled at the results.  Impressively realistic.  Now I'm eagerly awaiting the ability to share a Captured environment with others on their own headsets.  I have Quest-owning family members who now live far away in different countries, and plan to have us all meet up in our favourite, familiar rooms.  Exciting.
Scans take a while to process but the wait is worth it! Absolutely amazing results that truly feel like you’re actually there. Can’t wait to scan more locations.

I enjoy this idea and look forward to see where this goes. Is there a way to delete an environment once it's been created? If so,  how?  If not, please allow deleting an environment that the user created.

It is a wonderful and new way to save memories, I hope that it is possible to save it , process, and show on devices ,maybe not only on vr headsets on pc and mobile also and we able to take photos for objects like cars bikes and clothes that will give a huge penifits for online shopping

I scanned my own room yesterday and saw the result today, I was quite shocked how good it was, it worked extremely well, and the scale is 100% accurate to real life. The only negative is waiting a few hours for it to be processed in the cloud, and when viewing my room, I was about a foot taller than I should be.
The future is looking great, and it would be cool if we could scan people too, as long as we have their consent.

reviews.review_title: Not working with my 4G router, or with
reviews.review_description:
When v81 came out I checked my headset every single day. All I wanted was to use and experience the great Hyperscape app and get a glimpse of what that was all about.
Finally, I was prompted to install the update and I jumped straight into Hyperscape, but quickly I understood I had issues with it. I could never open one of the preexisting scanned rooms, but I was able to scan my own rooms and upload them, but not open them.
I have a 4G router at home as my only broadband for all my devices, and I have been using cellular broadband for many years and never have had issues. The speed is quite good.

The other day, I went outside to a 5G spot that I know, where I can get speeds around 600MB per sec, turned on my hotspot on my phone, and connected my headset to my hotspot at a blazing speed, but still I could not load the scanned rooms. The app just times out every single time...

The only way I got to test this was when I brought my headset to work and hid in the toilets so I could connect to WiFi and enjoy the experience of visualising the room I scanned at home and the other public ones.

It's nothing to do with the internet speed, but there is in fact some kind of block imposed by Meta that won't allow loading the scanned rooms if it detects that the network is based on cellular, either using a hotspot or even using a proper cellular WiFi router.

This is really a shame, and after all these weeks since I first got v81 installed, I haven't had the pleasure of showing my wife and kids any or the rooms I scanned in our house... I really wanted to show them, but I simply couldn't.
The only way would be bringing the headset to work or to somebody else's house and start putting the headset in my head, which is quite stupid. Simply doesn't make sense having to get out of home just to do that.

I hope this is fixed in the following app update. Please Meta??

Scanned my whole apt fine but uploading is killing me.
It seems like every time the Quest 3 sleeps the upload fails.
What seem to work (but didnt) was keeping it plugged in and setting the sleep and screen time to 4 hrs each. 
I wasn't home for 4hrs so I dunno how far it for uploading. Will try again later.....right now it's meh!

As far as I heard/ understand, the processing/ rendering of the scanned Hyperscape scene has been transferred from streaming over the User's network to being processed on the headset itself. 

IF that's the case, can an option be made available to choose to render A. over the network or B. on the headset. Maybe run a network test.

Let's not serve the least common denominator.
Meaning, I rather have a high quality Horizon Central style environment rather than a poor quality Hyperscape environment. 

Even though it's realistic, I don't want the Gaussian Splats to appear to be rendered poorly.

Developer Response would be highly appreciated. 

Thanks!

I haven't tried it yet. I just thought I'll get on here and ask the question? Maybe it's not the place to ask but I'm gonna ask anyways. Can humans pets be scaned aswell in certain places.
Like grandma in the rocking chair holding Mr. WhiskersI  but can humans be included in the the scan? If so, this would put a cherry 🍒🍓🫐🍍on top

Easy 5 star rating. 
Tried a few scans and you get better at it as you go. 
As well as scanning spaces inside and out you can also scan people in and if you can get them to stay still enough you have amazing life sized replicas of family members in their own environments you and your future family can experience for generations to come.
Game changer

This is a cool app, unfortunately, I can’t seem to figure out how to make what I’ve scanned discoverable.

I’m not above that I’m doing something stupid, but I can’t seem to find any solution…￼￼

Although it's still in the early stage, it's mind-blowing; there's a lot of potential within.
I hope we can have these updates in the near future.
☆ Interactive objects
☆ Animated environment (trees moving and maybe CGI birds in the sky) 
☆ Immersive sound effects for the particular space.
☆ Codec Avatars
Overall, I'm excited about the release of the invite function and want to see what else is in store.

I played with the preview last year, and have been *very* impressed with this.  I took my time doing one of my rooms and it came out amazing.  It had great detail, and if anything it looks better than the live pass through camera view on my Quest 3. 

The ability to invite your friends just got announced - I haven’t tried that yet but I love the concept.  I work for a library system and we have a few VR kits that we send out to the different locations, and it will be wild to share the hyperscape captures of these spaces as we send the kits around.

I tested the app and scanned a new construction build. The result was very impressive. I show cased the product to a client which was very impressed. But, tried to show case it to another client and the scan I uploaded was no longer available. I understand it is still in Beta Mode but think this product should be fully released soon. Without such glitches as I experience like my previous uploads no longer being available.

reviews.review_title: Take this immediately to the next level!
reviews.review_description: Take this immediately to the next level! PLEASE make our scans shareable so that I can invite others to be in the same ‘scanned’ environment together with our virtual avatars.  (A few prop seats  and or surfaces can be placed at each remote viewer’s offsite locations and the users can simulate the environment!)

Captured one room, I took my time to really see everything from every angle. Instructions are very clear and easy to follow. It took a couple hours until I got notified that my capture was ready but I was blown away by how realistic it looked.

I did had a problem with getting randomly teleported by a couple cm's.

Plus for those who complain the download button is greyed out or the app doesn't appear, it only showed up for me when I changed the app version to beta in the meta app on my phone. I was also struggling with getting it downloaded.

Im shocked this can be done with the quest 3.  How valuable it will be to show your kids  and grandkids where you used to live, Where you used to work,  The inside of ur car.  It looks so real and to scale. This app blindsided me, i never expected this.  Adds incredible value to the quest.
As someone else said, thank you meta.... i still dont forgive you for echo VR murder, but thanks for this

reviews.review_title: Encountered a problem that could not be
reviews.review_description:
I have been unable to upload for 3 consecutive days. Has anyone encountered the same problem?
It always shows that the upload failed, and there is no response after clicking. It is always ready to upload or upload failed.

Real estate – I can literally walk around my own yard like I’m actually there. It’s mind-blowing! Why spend hours browsing offers when you can explore them comfortably from your sofa, as if you were really there?

Selling things – furniture, cars, clothes, anything. It’s enough to help you make a pre-decision about whether you want to go further with the purchase. And if they make it multiplayer so buyers and sellers can talk in real time – that would be pure magic.

Capturing memories – weddings, birthdays, mountain views… it feels like you’re inside the photo. For me, this is the new camera for truly important moments I want to relive in the future.

Virtual tourism – art exhibitions, museums, landmarks, and places where people can gather and experience together.

Games - imagine running and shooting zombies in your own yard...

But by far the most exciting is the simulation of real environments. Right now it’s just still frames, but if it moves to 26 frames per second, NPCs powered by AI and headsets like the Quest 4 demo, the world as we know it will reach a whole new level of reality or insanity !

PS. Add the posibility to save OFFLINE maps and to add other users in to the session.

This app is amazing. The fidelity of the final scan is nearly indistinguishable from what you would get with pass through on the Quest 3. You can even see the environment outside open windows. I can't wait to be able to share these environments with friends or family. This could be an incredible way to share what my new house looks like with my mom who is disabled and can't come inside. Or, my kids could show their future kids their room that they grew up in. It's really like being back in the place.

reviews.review_title: Even in beta it is already fantastic!
reviews.review_description: Who knew that one of the best uses of VR would turn out to be reality?  Really impressed with the virtual version of my real game room!  It was a bit of a pain to keep my headset awake for the 20 minutes it took to upload, and my virtual head is a bit higher than my actual head in my real room, so it would be nice to be able to adjust that, but overall this is super impressive!

Very nice application. 


Can you add the possibility to see the window browser (Netflix, etc) in the environment that we capture?

Everything in the scanning process was flawless and my capture was absolutely incredible. I've used different gaussian methods as they have become available and this is by far the best I've used. I own and operate an augmented reality company and can see this being a serious tool us.

I can’t actually believe this is free as the potential is awesome. Not just in the vr playing world but for things like house sellers or hotels etc sending vr walkthroughs to customers.  
I tried this in my small office yesterday and was able to view it today. I was sat in the same place and when the scan came up it took me a minute to realise I was looking at the scan and not just the pass through image.  Which is very disorienting once you move from your position! 
 This sort of thing, for me at least is so much more worthwhile than the stupid ‘worlds’ they keep pushing.  They’re just a non realistic place full of screaming kids this on the other hand is awesome. Or at least it has the potential to be. Will be great if they give the option to share the scans. 
I’m just kinda expecting there to be a cost once it’s released. If there is Meta, it would be nice idea to allow all beta testers to keep it free?

I tried Hyperscape Capture in my apartment and also outdoors, and the results were simply breathtaking. I would never have thought that a Meta Quest 3 would allow you to scan your surroundings in such detail and transfer them into virtual reality. I hope Meta continues to develop this technology and incorporates it into a future Meta Quest 4.

As someone who has used VR regularly for over a decade it's rare to be wowed anymore but this app is legitimately incredible. I scanned a room in my house and it's so photorealistic its hard to put into words. Do NOT let the low ratings discourage you from downloading this because they're all from people who can't get it to work for whatever reason LOL

Ive wanted this technology ever since I first saw matterport.

The idea of 3d scanning a location and time and being able to visit it when ever I want is magic.

The 3 step process is very simple.

I scanned my daughters bedroom because im half way through a DIY project and my back garden.

It took me about 10/15 minute a scan and then  it took maybe 3 hours to process on metas servers.

To say im impressed with the results from my first attempt is an understatement, its incredible. 

I plan on doing scans of all seasons in the garden and all the holidays like Christmas and Halloween in our decorated living space. I also plan on taking the quest on holiday to scan some select locations like balconies or queit coves.

Im hoping meta eventually enable the ability to change lighting  and it would be great if you could upload a looping audio clip to add to the immersion. 

I honestly think this app will sell headsets for this alone. 


Thank you Meta

reviews.review_title: Need access not from device owner
reviews.review_description: My wife set herself as device owner and I can't from my profile enter the app :/ it's weird... . Fix that please !

The start is really good and actually deserves five stars. However, I still miss some features that I would like to see included and that would be very helpful to all users.

Sharing worlds:
You should be able to keep your worlds private, as is currently the case, or make them accessible to individual people, friends in general, or even the public.

Labeling:
I would like to be able to write on windows, doors, walls, tables, and generally on smooth surfaces in different colors and sizes.
For example, something like “Stairwell closed” or “Apartment is 100 square meters.”

Hide areas:
If I record my entire apartment, for example, people who see it could see my fiber optic ID. This is nobody's business, and I would like to be able to draw over the area in black with a pen, for example. Or this pen could blur and make illegible areas that should not be seen, such as writing that is nobody's business.

Surcharge/costs:
I would like to politely request that when this app is finally released, it be sold as a one-time purchase rather than a subscription model with monthly or annual payments.

Cost distribution:
For example, the normal app as it is now designed could be sold for $25 with only the publish and share functions included. If you also want to edit your worlds, for example

Translated with Deep.

your stupid app messed my Q3 up so bad i had to do a reset, menus, room scan, had to take the batteries out of controllers, its like you could care less if you ever get repeat Custom again. Really bad attempt.

This is a mind blowing tech demo showing what is possible with Meta quest hardware and API.

To make it truly immersive, Meta should consider allowing some degree of configurability, because I have found that the floor level seems to be slightly off...i.e. if you are within a room scale boundary and you load a scanned room, the floor of the virtual room is slightly below the physical floor, making it seem like you are a bit taller than in real life. It's more problematic if you are in a temporary stationary boundary, the floor levels become wacky. It would be great for there to be a user-selectable adjustment for floor level.
The upload process does not have a pause feature. So with fhe slightest hiccup your upload resets!

reviews.review_title: potentially very good, but not yet
reviews.review_description:
Scan quality is very high with respect Into The Scaniverse, but with a major drawback: unlike scaniverse, you can't open browser or any other 2d app in the 3d environments. I like so much reading books and newspapers in 3d scans made with Scaniverse!

Another major drawback, already solved in Scaniverse: why do I need connection during scan? Scan can't start without connection, and if you start when connection is available, it anyway fails in case of loss of connection... for no reason! Just let us do outdoor scans without connection, we will upload them later!!

And please make the splats available as 3d environments for immersive home.
Spending time with friends in a familiar place is nothing short of remarkable.
It was amazing to be able to walk through my house, and share it to others. I see many possible uses for this.

reviews.review_title: No way to recreate a world
reviews.review_description:
I scanned my room. Uploaded a scan. Waited for it to process and mistakenly deleted Hyperscape Preview, unaware that Hyperscape Capture relies on it to function correctly. As a result, I had to reinstall Preview. Unfortunately, my scan still fails, stating "World cannot be created", and the only solution is to delete the scan entirely, which I would prefer to avoid. 

Unfortunately there's no way to re-upload the scan, try creating this world again, or some similar option.

4 stars because this is very cool idea. Unfortunately it fails to finish upload and I can't restart even after restarting device. It also now spins up the vent and battery seems to empty fast. Please add option to continue upload.
